prompt: Invent an original, vivid metaphor for the experience of learning a new language as an adult. Explain it in three sentences.
response: Learning a new language as an adult is like trying to knit a complex tapestry with only the occasional, fleeting memory of how to hold the needles. Each new stitch (word) feels foreign and clumsy, but with persistent unraveling and re-stitching of threads (grammar), the fabric slowly begins to form a recognizable pattern, eventually allowing you to weave your own colorful stories. You're not simply re-learning how to knit; you're discovering entirely new colors, textures, and designs that transform your perception of the world.
